title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Automation and Data Client Systems · Failure Triage
summary: A data reliability engineer tracing a failed overnight import must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for retry safety, replayability, backoff, jitter, and retry budgets, producing bounded retry and replay decision engine. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded retry and replay decision engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression r…
objectives: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Prove request bodies and credential state can be reconstructed before replaying an attempt. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Retryable Classification
steps.instruction:
debug case — overnight ingestion repair client sees one reset, Retry-After:4, then 503 while a nested helper also retries. Prove method and body replayability, cap attempts and elapsed budget, apply deterministic jitter, and prevent duplicate or amplified work. Competency probe: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
